title: SQL Repositories, Pools, Transactions, and Request Consistency · Mastery Check
summary: A production-readiness review compares near-miss decisions for sql repositories, pools, transactions, and request consistency and requires the smallest safe context-bound repository and transaction contract with changed-request evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for context-bound repository and transaction contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat sql.DB as a concurrent pool, open once, verify readiness, tune open and idle capacity and lifetime, observe Stats, and close at process shutdown.
Use dialect parameters, QueryContext, Scan, rows Close and Err, bounded result sets, and error identity without constructing SQL from input.
Define atomic invariants, BeginTx with context and isolation, defer rollback, commit exactly once, and handle PostgreSQL Read Committed changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
